campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to hear updates on transit authority finances, review how the city's money was invested in March, and adopt a new policy about what the city won't allow on its properties. The council also discussed efforts to fill open city jobs and keep current employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority financial report for March 2026
New city policy restricting use of city property for federal immigration enforcement
Open positions in city government and plans to recruit and retain staff

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could comment on a proposed new rule (ordinance). After hearing public input, the council moved to fast-track the ordinance by skipping the usual first reading step, so they can vote on officially adopting it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
Ordinance first reading waived to expedite approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from their previous session and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign several construction and professional service contracts to move forward with town improvements.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and closed session records
Reviewed city's financial performance and investments for February 2026
Authorized construction contracts with multiple vendors (QLM, Inc. and others)
Approved three-year professional services agreement
Directed preparation of town improvement project descriptions

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several routine matters, including updating official meeting records and moving forward with replacing an elevator at the Terminal building. The council also discussed a plan to hire companies to clean up homeless encampments, approved a deal with Clear Channel for digital advertising signs on city property, and authorized spending city money on a firefighter memorial event.
keyTopics: Terminal building elevator replacement project moving forward
New contracts being considered for encampment cleanup services
Digital billboard agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC
City funding approved for 'Light the Night' fallen firefighters memorial event

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes, hear from residents during public comment, and decide on neighborhood grant funding for the 2026-27 budget year. The council reviewed which community projects and neighborhood initiatives should receive city funding and discussed attendance at neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant applications and funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comment period for residents
Meeting minutes approval
City attendance and engagement at neighborhood meetings

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were given opportunities to participate both in person and online to watch the proceedings and provide public comment.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period for residents
In-person and online participation options
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council met to discuss purchasing or negotiating a deal for a property at 10480 Finch Avenue with the Cupertino Union School District. City Manager Tina Kapoor and City Attorney Floy Andrews led negotiations focused on figuring out the price and payment terms. The council also reviewed a program called Silicon Valley Hopper and discussed plans for getting grant funding.
keyTopics: Purchase or lease negotiation for 10480 Finch Avenue property
Price and payment terms being worked out with the school district
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and future grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The council also approved a new contract with the firefighters union covering 2026-2029, though one item was withdrawn before discussion.
keyTopics: Large promotional signs permitted near Levi's Stadium during World Cup 2026
New firefighters union contract approved for 2026-2029
One agenda item withdrawn by staff before being discussed

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas City Council met to plan the city's finances and development for the next several years. The main items included approving a five-year spending plan for major projects, reviewing the upcoming year's operating budget, finalizing a plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, and updating how the City Council runs its meetings.
keyTopics: 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan - major city projects and infrastructure spending
FY 2026-27 Operating Budget - how the city will spend money day-to-day
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan - new recreational spaces coming to that area
City Council procedures update - changes to how meetings are conducted

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss a proposal to divide a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into nine separate parcels for building nine new houses and seven smaller accessory homes. The council also handled routine business like electing leadership positions and reviewing the director's schedule.
keyTopics: Plan to subdivide Old Trace Road property into 9 residential lots plus accessory units
Creation of new private street to serve the nine homes
Election of council chair and vice chair positions
Director's report and meeting schedule updates
